message:
feat(core): wire live tiered scrollback telemetry into fleet memory management

The fleet scrollback coordinator, runtime maintenance loop, and sharding
layer now consume real tiered scrollback telemetry from the mux backend
(warm resident bytes, warm resident lines, in-memory scrollback rows)
instead of reporting hardcoded zeros for warm_bytes and warm_pages.

This enables the fleet memory controller to make informed eviction
decisions based on actual mux-side memory pressure rather than only
arena-tracked bytes.

- WeztermInterface gains pane_tiered_scrollback_summary() with default
  Ok(None) implementation
- WeztermClient implements it via mux_pool.get_pane_render_changes()
  behind the vendored feature gate
- Runtime maintenance loop collects summaries once per tick and merges
  them into PaneScrollbackInfo used by the coordinator
- Eviction accounting in proportional and emergency paths fixed:
  targets_applied now only increments when pages/bytes were actually
  evicted (not when a no-op eviction touched a null/empty pane)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
